entries.title: Cyclospora outbreaks causing diarrhea in US expected to continue through August
entries.description: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention says it is scouring food supply chain for culprit, which may be lettuceWhat is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea?Federal health officials have said they expect the outbreaks of cyclospora – a parasite that causes watery, explosive diarrhea – across the US to continue through August as they scour the food supply chain for the culprit.The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has identified 1,645 lab-confirmed cases of the parasite; reported that 145 people have been hospitalized; and reported that they have a backlog of 5,100 cases that require more analysis, including interviews. Continue reading...

entries.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
entries.description: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.

entries.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
entries.description: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

entries.title: Space-based aspect of Trump's 'Golden Dome' shield gets boost with $1.75 billion in new contracts
entries.description: The Trump administration's Golden Dome missile defense shield project is expanding its secretive network of space-based, missile-detecting satellites with a $1.75 billion dual contract for two defense technology firms, despite concerns on Capitol Hill about ballooning costs.

entries.title: Houston prosecutor says ICE tactics "in no way resemble" traditional policing
entries.description: Asked about the recent ICE-related fatal shootings, Harris District Attorney Sean Teare says the actions of federal immigration agents "in no way resemble" the tactics of "every law enforcement agency" he's worked for.

entries.title: U.S. resumes naval blockade against Iran, launches new airstrikes
entries.description: The United States on Tuesday resumed its naval blockade against Iran and launched new strikes against the country. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports and retired Col. Mark Cancian joins with analysis.

entries.title: WGA sues Paramount, claiming Warner Bros. acquisition would take away jobs
entries.description: The Writers Guild of America sued Paramount on Tuesday, alleging its planned acquisition of Warner Bros. violates federal antitrust law. The lawsuit comes a day after California Atty. Gen. Rob Bonta and 11 other state attorneys general sued Paramount.

entries.title: Key Moments: Supreme Court Justices Barrett and Kagan Testify Before Congress
entries.description: The high court sent a delegation to present a budget request and answer lawmakers’ questions for the first time since 2019.

entries.title: New York City Officials Inspecting Other Buildings After Midtown High Rise Buckled
entries.description: The buildings department is looking at projects of those involved in the Manhattan incident last week.

entries.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entries.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entries.title: Iraq seizes mounds of gold and millions in cash in corruption crackdown
entries.description: An anti-corruption probe in Iraq leads to seizure of 825 pounds of gold and tens of millions in cash from lawmakers and government officials.

entries.title: "Miracle on the Hudson" pilot, Capt. "Sully," shares Alzheimer's diagnosis
entries.description: Capt. Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger, who famously landed a passenger jet on the Hudson River in 2009, said he has been diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease.

entries.title: Russian Soyuz rocket launches fresh crew to space station
entries.description: The new crew will replace three other Soyuz fliers wrapping up a 240-day stay aboard the International Space Station.

entries.title: Spain reaches World Cup final with dominant 2-0 win over France
entries.description: In a match Tuesday featuring two of soccer's biggest heavyweights, Spain put in a masterful performance, frustrating France to the tune of a 2-0 win to advance to Sunday's World Cup final​.

entries.title: New diagnoses slow in NYC Legionnaires' disease outbreak; source still unclear
entries.description: A Legionnaires' disease outbreak in a New York City neighborhood now counts 60 cases, but new diagnoses are slowing, health officials said Tuesday. They reported progress on inspections for the disease-causing bacteria but still haven't pinpointed a source.

entries.title: Chelsea in advanced talks to sign Manchester United’s Melvine Malard
entries.description: Chelsea are in advanced talks over a transfer for Manchester United forward Melvine Malard. Talks are taking place between the clubs over a deal which would be worth in the region of £850,000 plus add-ons, with personal terms not expected to be an issue. It would represent a club-record sale for United and the highest fee a women’s club has received for a player with a year left on their contract. entries.title: Spain deliver World Cup masterclass against France to reach final
entries.description: Spain ended France's dream of a third World Cup triumph on Tuesday, taming their star-studded attack in a 2-0 victory to reach the final against England or Argentina.The encounter takes place 40 years after Diego Maradona's infamous "Hand of God" goal helped Argentina to a 2-1 win at the 1986 World Cup in Mexico.
